_comment: Aspect labels and descriptions. Mirrors `locales/cards/en.json`: top-level keys are aspect NAMES (the unique `id.json`-style key under any section of `cards/aspects.json` — `wood`, `pine`, `corpus+`, `crafting`, …). Aspect names are globally unique across the `aspects` / `features` / `traits` sections, so this catalog is FLAT by name — no section nesting — which matches how aspects resolve (id → name via `aspectInfo`). Each leaf is `{ label, description: { simple } }`; `label` is REQUIRED by the loader (an object with no `label` is treated as an intermediate path, not an entry). `description.simple` is the only slot consumed today. Inheritance is NOT replicated here: a sub-aspect that inherited its parent's text in `aspects.json` (e.g. `berry` ← `food`, `fuel` ← `fire`) carries NO `description` block — `aspectDescription` walks the parent chain and resolves it from the ancestor. Empty roots (`anima`, `sollertia`) likewise carry label only.

description.simple: Timber — logs, branches, raw lumber
Pine — fast-growing softwood
Rock, ore, mineral material
Processed or smelted metallic material
Sustenance, edible produce
Living plant matter — trees, bushes, undergrowth
Brush — tangled undergrowth
Combustible energy source
Fresh water — drinking, irrigation, flow
Remains — a spent body, suitable as a recipe input for rituals and similar story beats
Injuries — wounds, broken bones, lasting harm
Enhanced corpus — vigor, heightened resilience
The body — physical capacity and health
Fatigue — exhaustion, transient depletion
Standard corpus — baseline vitality
Spiritual or willful contribution
Spiritual or willful contribution
Construction and fabrication — building, shaping, assembling
Builder — assembles structures from materials
Temporary — expires after a duration
Tier / rank — used as a render index by cards whose object pack stores one variant per level (e.g. the alter tile).

Faction allegiance — sub-features pick a specific faction folder. A card carrying any descendant of `faction` renders with that descendant's folder (e.g. `master/<object>/chorus/`) instead of the local player's faction. Lets a designer ship faction-specific cards that look the same regardless of who holds them.
Chorus faction.
Chord faction.
Resonance faction.
Movement allowance per turn. Spent against tile `cost` when the soul traverses the map.
Has an inventory — click the card to open the bag of cards owned by it.
Movement cost paid to enter the tile. Higher values represent rougher or slower terrain.
Spatial elevation of the tile — affects lighting, reach, and terrain layering. Per-tile, not summed across a chain.
Lower bound (inclusive) of the elevation envelope. [0, 1]. Cells whose sampled elevation falls below this exclude the tile from the candidate set. Omitted ⇒ 0 (no lower bound).
Upper bound (inclusive) of the elevation envelope. [0, 1]. Omitted ⇒ 1 (no upper bound).
Lower bound (inclusive) of the temperature envelope. [0, 1]. 0 = tundra, 1 = desert. Omitted ⇒ 0.
Upper bound (inclusive) of the temperature envelope. [0, 1]. Omitted ⇒ 1.
Lower bound (inclusive) of the humidity envelope. [0, 1]. 0 = arid, 1 = saturated. Omitted ⇒ 0.
Upper bound (inclusive) of the humidity envelope. [0, 1]. Omitted ⇒ 1.
Lower bound (inclusive) of the aether-saturation envelope. [0, 1]. 0 = mundane, 1 = ley-line / resonance site. Omitted ⇒ 0.
Upper bound (inclusive) of the aether-saturation envelope. [0, 1]. Omitted ⇒ 1.
Relative weight when multiple tiles share a cell's candidate set. Default 1.0.
Hex coverage role. 0 = decorator overlay, 1 = base tile (fills the hex). Read as boolean (`>= 0.5` is base). Omitted ⇒ 1.
Cluster group id — tiles sharing a non-zero value bias toward spawning adjacent to one another. 0 = no clustering (default).
How much the cluster bias overrides the climate-only candidate roll. [0, 1]. 0 = clustering ignored; 1 = if any neighbor shares this group, this tile wins. Omitted ⇒ 0.
